requirement.statement: 系统 SHALL require an owned runtime-control checkpoint before recovering an AI runtime session in production load or resume paths.

case.given: A session contains runtime-control upgrade metadata, a checkpoint commit marker, matching real durable heads, and clean effect evidence
case.when: The runtime loads or resumes the session
case.then: The system SHALL pass the recovery gate and continue VM recovery

case.given: A materialized old session has no runtime-control upgrade metadata or checkpoint marker
case.when: The runtime attempts production load or resume without running session-upgrade
case.then: The system SHALL reject recovery with an explicit upgrade-required error

case.given: A session has mismatched heads, orphaned tool output, dirty effect evidence, or removed handler evidence
case.when: The runtime attempts load or resume
case.then: The system SHALL reject recovery and expose recovery blockers

requirement.statement: 系统 SHALL make the runtime-control engine the owner of AI runtime checkpoint save consistency.

case.given: The runtime reaches a snapshot safepoint
case.then: The system SHALL submit a runtime-control checkpoint command/effect
The system SHALL run concrete conversation, transcript, snapshot, and manifest writes only as support effects
The system SHALL commit the checkpoint cohort only after real heads match the written files

case.given: The runtime has mandatory mailbox work, half-started tool call, or unpaired completion work
case.then: The system SHALL NOT write a new authoritative checkpoint commit marker

requirement.statement: 系统 SHALL route runtime effect lifecycle evidence through the runtime-control engine facade and SHALL NOT let executor or support code directly own evidence persistence.

case.given: A provider, tool, MCP, bash, permission, questionnaire, or async completion event occurs
case.then: The system SHALL record request/result/waiting evidence through the runtime-control facade
Recovery SHALL classify unpaired result evidence as orphaned or dirty

requirement.statement: 系统 SHALL keep rebuildable projections and append-only journal sinks outside the authoritative checkpoint cohort unless a future spec explicitly changes their role.

case.given: Derived indexes are missing, stale, or partially written
case.when: The runtime recovers from a clean owned checkpoint
case.then: The system SHALL treat derived indexes as projection cache and rebuild or refresh them without failing recovery

case.given: Ingress or diagnostics XNL logs contain entries beyond or outside a checkpoint
case.then: The system SHALL NOT block checkpoint recovery solely because journal sink event counts differ
Session upgrade SHALL preserve existing journal sink files without converting them into checkpoint-owned records
